The main claim is: Whether deceptive alignment arises in the high-path-dependence regime hinges on how 'sticky' a model's existing proxy goals are — whether gradient descent repurposes them into long-term goals or replaces them with a pointer to the training objective.
Goal stickiness is testable with current systems, and as training objectives grow more complex deception likely wins almost regardless of the baseline stickiness level.
